<path>/Users/dstern/src/personal/opencode-relay/opencode-relay/.worktrees/ticket-6.2/src/lib/client-init.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: // ─── Client Init (Ticket 3.6) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Handles the initial handshake when a browser client connects via WebSocket.
3: // Sends session info (with cached events or REST API history), model info,
4: // agent list, provider/model list, and PTY replay to the new client.
5: //
6: // Extracted from relay-stack.ts's `client_connected` handler so the logic is
7: // independently testable and relay-stack stays slim.
8: 
9: import { formatErrorDetail, RelayError } from "./errors.js";
10: import { filterAgents } from "./handlers/index.js";
11: import type { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
12: import type { OpenCodeClient } from "./opencode-client.js";
13: import type { PermissionBridge } from "./permission-bridge.js";
14: import type { PtyManager } from "./pty-manager.js";
15: import type { QuestionBridge } from "./question-bridge.js";
16: import type { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
17: import type { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
18: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
19: 
20: // ─── Dependencies ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21: 
22: export interface ClientInitDeps {
23: 	wsHandler: {
24: 		broadcast: (msg: RelayMessage) => void;
25: 		sendTo: (clientId: string, msg: RelayMessage) => void;
26: 	};
27: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
28: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
29: 	messageCache: MessageCache;
30: 	overrides: SessionOverrides;
31: 	ptyManager: PtyManager;
32: 	permissionBridge: Pick<PermissionBridge, "getPending">;
33: 	questionBridge: Pick<QuestionBridge, "getPending">;
34: 	log: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
35: }
36: 
37: // ─── Handler ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
38: 
39: /**
40:  * Handle a newly connected browser client. Sends all initial state:
41:  * - Active session with cached events or REST API history
42:  * - Session list
43:  * - Model info (from session or overrides)
44:  * - Agent list (filtered)
45:  * - Provider/model list (connected only)
46:  * - PTY list + scrollback replay
47:  *
48:  * Errors are sent as INIT_FAILED messages without crashing the handler.
49:  */
50: export async function handleClientConnected(
51: 	deps: ClientInitDeps,
52: 	clientId: string,
53: ): Promise<void> {
54: 	const {
55: 		wsHandler,
56: 		client,
57: 		sessionMgr,
58: 		messageCache,
59: 		overrides,
60: 		ptyManager,
61: 		permissionBridge,
62: 		questionBridge,
63: 	} = deps;
64: 
65: 	const sendInitError = (err: unknown, prefix: string) => {
66: 		deps.log(`   [ws] ${prefix}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`);
67: 		wsHandler.sendTo(
68: 			clientId,
69: 			RelayError.fromCaught(err, "INIT_FAILED", prefix).toMessage(),
70: 		);
71: 	};
72: 
73: 	// ── Active session with event replay ─────────────────────────────────
74: 	const activeId = sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId();
75: 	if (activeId) {
76: 		const events = messageCache.getEvents(activeId);
77: 		const hasChatContent =
78: 			events?.some((e) => e.type === "user_message" || e.type === "delta") ??
79: 			false;
80: 		if (events && hasChatContent) {
81: 			// Cache hit with content: include raw events for client replay
82: 			wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
83: 				type: "session_switched",
84: 				id: activeId,
85: 				events,
86: 			});
87: 		} else {
88: 			// Cache miss (session from before relay started): REST API fallback
89: 			try {
90: 				const history = await sessionMgr.loadHistory(activeId);
91: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
92: 					type: "session_switched",
93: 					id: activeId,
94: 					history: {
95: 						messages: history.messages,
96: 						hasMore: history.hasMore,
97: 						total: history.total,
98: 					},
99: 				});
100: 			} catch {
101: 				// If REST API fails too, just send the switch without history
102: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
103: 					type: "session_switched",
104: 					id: activeId,
105: 				});
106: 			}
107: 		}
108: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "status", status: "idle" });
109: 
110: 		// Send model/agent info from the active session
111: 		try {
112: 			const session = await client.getSession(activeId);
113: 			if (session.modelID) {
114: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
115: 					type: "model_info",
116: 					model: session.modelID,
117: 					provider: session.providerID ?? "",
118: 				});
119: 			} else if (overrides.model) {
120: 				// Session has no model set, but relay has a selected model
121: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
122: 					type: "model_info",
123: 					model: overrides.model.modelID,
124: 					provider: overrides.model.providerID,
125: 				});
126: 			}
127: 		} catch (err) {
128: 			sendInitError(err, "Failed to load session info");
129: 			if (overrides.model) {
130: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
131: 					type: "model_info",
132: 					model: overrides.model.modelID,
133: 					provider: overrides.model.providerID,
134: 				});
135: 			}
136: 		}
137: 	}
138: 
139: 	// ── Session list ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
140: 	try {
141: 		const sessions = await sessionMgr.listSessions();
142: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "session_list", sessions });
143: 	} catch (err) {
144: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list sessions");
145: 	}
146: 
147: 	// ── Pending permissions + questions (reconnect replay) ───────────────
148: 	for (const perm of permissionBridge.getPending()) {
149: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
150: 			type: "permission_request",
151: 			requestId: perm.requestId,
152: 			toolName: perm.toolName,
153: 			toolInput: perm.toolInput,
154: 		});
155: 	}
156: 	for (const q of questionBridge.getPending()) {
157: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
158: 			type: "ask_user",
159: 			toolId: q.toolId,
160: 			questions: q.questions,
161: 		});
162: 	}
163: 
164: 	// ── Agent list (filter out internal agents) ──────────────────────────
165: 	try {
166: 		const rawAgents = await client.listAgents();
167: 		const agents = filterAgents(rawAgents);
168: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "agent_list", agents });
169: 	} catch (err) {
170: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list agents");
171: 	}
172: 
173: 	// ── Provider/model list + auto-select default ────────────────────────
174: 	try {
175: 		const providerResult = await client.listProviders();
176: 		const connectedSet = new Set(providerResult.connected);
177: 		const providers = providerResult.providers
178: 			.map((p) => ({
179: 				id: p.id || p.name || "",
180: 				name: p.name || p.id || "",
181: 				configured: connectedSet.has(p.id) || connectedSet.has(p.name),
182: 				models: (p.models ?? []).map((m) => ({
183: 					id: m.id,
184: 					name: m.name || m.id,
185: 					provider: p.id || p.name || "",
186: 				})),
187: 			}))
188: 			.filter((p) => p.configured);
189: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "model_list", providers });
190: 
191: 		// Auto-select default model from a connected provider if none set
192: 		if (!overrides.model) {
193: 			for (const providerId of providerResult.connected) {
194: 				const defaultModelId = providerResult.defaults[providerId];
195: 				if (defaultModelId) {
196: 					overrides.setModelDefault({
197: 						providerID: providerId,
198: 						modelID: defaultModelId,
199: 					});
200: 					wsHandler.broadcast({
201: 						type: "model_info",
202: 						model: defaultModelId,
203: 						provider: providerId,
204: 					});
205: 					deps.log(
206: 						`   [model] Auto-selected default: ${defaultModelId} (${providerId})`,
207: 					);
208: 					break;
209: 				}
210: 			}
211: 		}
212: 	} catch (err) {
213: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list providers");
214: 	}
215: 
216: 	// ── PTY list + scrollback replay ─────────────────────────────────────
217: 	if (ptyManager.sessionCount > 0) {
218: 		const ptys = ptyManager.listSessions();
219: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "pty_list", ptys });
220: 		// Replay scrollback for each PTY to this specific client
221: 		for (const { id: ptyId } of ptys) {
222: 			const scrollback = ptyManager.getScrollback(ptyId);
223: 			if (scrollback) {
224: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
225: 					type: "pty_output",
226: 					ptyId,
227: 					data: scrollback,
228: 				});
229: 			}
230: 			const session = ptyManager.getSession(ptyId);
231: 			if (session?.exited) {
232: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
233: 					type: "pty_exited",
234: 					ptyId,
235: 					exitCode: session.exitCode ?? 0,
236: 				});
237: 			}
238: 		}
239: 	}
240: }

(End of file - total 240 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/dstern/src/personal/opencode-relay/opencode-relay/.worktrees/ticket-6.2/src/lib/permission-bridge.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: // ─── Permission Bridge (Ticket 1.5) ─────────────────────────────────────────
2: 
3: import type {
4: 	FrontendDecision,
5: 	OpenCodeDecision,
6: 	OpenCodeEvent,
7: 	PendingPermission,
8: } from "./types.js";
9: 
10: // ─── Decision Mapping ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11: 
12: const DECISION_MAP: Record<FrontendDecision, OpenCodeDecision> = {
13: 	allow: "once",
14: 	deny: "reject",
15: 	allow_always: "always",
16: };
17: 
18: const REVERSE_DECISION_MAP: Record<OpenCodeDecision, FrontendDecision> = {
19: 	once: "allow",
20: 	reject: "deny",
21: 	always: "allow_always",
22: };
23: 
24: /** Map frontend decision vocabulary → OpenCode vocabulary */
25: export function mapDecision(frontend: string): OpenCodeDecision | null {
26: 	return DECISION_MAP[frontend as FrontendDecision] ?? null;
27: }
28: 
29: /** Map OpenCode decision vocabulary → frontend vocabulary */
30: export function mapDecisionReverse(opencode: string): FrontendDecision | null {
31: 	return REVERSE_DECISION_MAP[opencode as OpenCodeDecision] ?? null;
32: }
33: 
34: // ─── Permission Bridge ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35: 
36: export interface PermissionBridgeOptions {
37: 	timeoutMs?: number;
38: 	now?: () => number;
39: }
40: 
41: export class PermissionBridge {
42: 	private pending: Map<string, PendingPermission> = new Map();
43: 	private alwaysAllowed: Set<string> = new Set();
44: 	private readonly timeoutMs: number;
45: 	private readonly now: () => number;
46: 
47: 	constructor(options: PermissionBridgeOptions = {}) {
48: 		this.timeoutMs = options.timeoutMs ?? 5 * 60_000; // 5 minutes
49: 		this.now = options.now ?? Date.now;
50: 	}
51: 
52: 	/** Process an incoming permission.asked SSE event */
53: 	onPermissionRequest(event: OpenCodeEvent): PendingPermission | null {
54: 		const props = event.properties as {
55: 			id?: string;
56: 			permission?: string;
57: 			patterns?: string[];
58: 			metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
59: 			always?: string[];
60: 		};
61: 
62: 		if (!props.id || !props.permission) return null;
63: 
64: 		// Auto-approve if tool is in the always-allowed set
65: 		if (this.alwaysAllowed.has(props.permission)) {
66: 			return null; // Caller should auto-reply
67: 		}
68: 
69: 		const entry: PendingPermission = {
70: 			requestId: props.id,
71: 			toolName: props.permission,
72: 			toolInput: {
73: 				patterns: props.patterns ?? [],
74: 				metadata: props.metadata ?? {},
75: 			},
76: 			always: props.always ?? [],
77: 			timestamp: this.now(),
78: 		};
79: 
80: 		this.pending.set(props.id, entry);
81: 		return entry;
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	/** Process a user's permission decision from the browser */
85: 	onPermissionResponse(
86: 		requestId: string,
87: 		decision: string,
88: 	): { mapped: OpenCodeDecision; toolName: string } | null {
89: 		const entry = this.pending.get(requestId);
90: 		if (!entry) return null; // Already resolved or unknown — ignore duplicate
91: 
92: 		const mapped = mapDecision(decision);
93: 		if (!mapped) return null;
94: 
95: 		// Track "always" decisions
96: 		if (mapped === "always") {
97: 			this.alwaysAllowed.add(entry.toolName);
98: 		}
99: 
100: 		this.pending.delete(requestId);
101: 		return { mapped, toolName: entry.toolName };
102: 	}
103: 
104: 	/** Handle a permission.replied SSE event (resolved from CLI) */
105: 	onPermissionReplied(requestId: string): boolean {
106: 		return this.pending.delete(requestId);
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	/** Get all pending permissions (for replay on reconnect) */
110: 	getPending(): PendingPermission[] {
111: 		return Array.from(this.pending.values());
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	/** Check if a tool is in the always-allowed set */
115: 	isAlwaysAllowed(toolName: string): boolean {
116: 		return this.alwaysAllowed.has(toolName);
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	/** Clear the always-allowed cache */
120: 	clearAlwaysAllowed(): void {
121: 		this.alwaysAllowed.clear();
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	/** Check for timed-out permissions and return their IDs */
125: 	checkTimeouts(): string[] {
126: 		const now = this.now();
127: 		const timedOut: string[] = [];
128: 
129: 		for (const [id, entry] of this.pending) {
130: 			if (now - entry.timestamp >= this.timeoutMs) {
131: 				timedOut.push(id);
132: 				this.pending.delete(id);
133: 			}
134: 		}
135: 
136: 		return timedOut;
137: 	}
138: 
139: 	/** Recover pending permissions from REST API response */
140: 	recoverPending(
141: 		permissions: Array<{
142: 			id: string;
143: 			permission: string;
144: 			patterns?: string[];
145: 			metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
146: 			always?: string[];
147: 		}>,
148: 	): PendingPermission[] {
149: 		const recovered: PendingPermission[] = [];
150: 		for (const p of permissions) {
151: 			const entry: PendingPermission = {
152: 				requestId: p.id,
153: 				toolName: p.permission,
154: 				toolInput: {
155: 					patterns: p.patterns ?? [],
156: 					metadata: p.metadata ?? {},
157: 				},
158: 				always: p.always ?? [],
159: 				timestamp: this.now(),
160: 			};
161: 			this.pending.set(p.id, entry);
162: 			recovered.push(entry);
163: 		}
164: 		return recovered;
165: 	}
166: 
167: 	/** Number of pending permissions */
168: 	get size(): number {
169: 		return this.pending.size;
170: 	}
171: }

(End of file - total 171 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/dstern/src/personal/opencode-relay/opencode-relay/.worktrees/ticket-6.2/src/lib/question-bridge.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: // ─── Question / Ask-User Bridge (Ticket 1.6) ────────────────────────────────
2: 
3: import type { AskUserQuestion, OpenCodeEvent } from "./types.js";
4: 
5: export interface PendingQuestion {
6: 	toolId: string;
7: 	questions: AskUserQuestion[];
8: 	timestamp: number;
9: }
10: 
11: export interface QuestionBridgeOptions {
12: 	timeoutMs?: number;
13: 	now?: () => number;
14: }
15: 
16: /**
17:  * Convert browser answer format to OpenCode's format.
18:  * Browser: { "0": "Option A", "1": "Option B" }
19:  * OpenCode: [["Option A"], ["Option B"]]
20:  *
21:  * For multi-select:
22:  * Browser: { "0": "Opt A, Opt B" }
23:  * OpenCode: [["Opt A", "Opt B"]]
24:  */
25: export function convertAnswers(
26: 	answers: Record<string, string>,
27: 	questions: AskUserQuestion[],
28: ): string[][] {
29: 	const result: string[][] = [];
30: 
31: 	for (let i = 0; i < questions.length; i++) {
32: 		const key = String(i);
33: 		const raw = answers[key];
34: 
35: 		if (raw === undefined || raw === "") {
36: 			result.push([]);
37: 			continue;
38: 		}
39: 
40: 		if (questions[i]!.multiSelect) {
41: 			// Split comma-separated selections
42: 			result.push(
43: 				raw
44: 					.split(",")
45: 					.map((s) => s.trim())
46: 					.filter((s) => s.length > 0),
47: 			);
48: 		} else {
49: 			result.push([raw]);
50: 		}
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	return result;
54: }
55: 
56: /**
57:  * Map OpenCode question format (with `multiple` field) to frontend format (with `multiSelect` field).
58:  */
59: export function mapQuestionFields(
60: 	ocQuestions: Array<{
61: 		question?: string;
62: 		header?: string;
63: 		options?: Array<{ label?: string; description?: string }>;
64: 		multiple?: boolean;
65: 		custom?: boolean;
66: 	}>,
67: ): AskUserQuestion[] {
68: 	return ocQuestions.map((q) => ({
69: 		question: q.question ?? "",
70: 		header: q.header ?? "",
71: 		options: (q.options ?? []).map((o) => ({
72: 			label: o.label ?? "",
73: 			description: o.description ?? "",
74: 		})),
75: 		multiSelect: q.multiple ?? false,
76: 		custom: q.custom ?? true,
77: 	}));
78: }
79: 
80: export class QuestionBridge {
81: 	private pending: Map<string, PendingQuestion> = new Map();
82: 	private readonly timeoutMs: number;
83: 	private readonly now: () => number;
84: 
85: 	constructor(options: QuestionBridgeOptions = {}) {
86: 		this.timeoutMs = options.timeoutMs ?? 10 * 60_000; // 10 minutes
87: 		this.now = options.now ?? Date.now;
88: 	}
89: 
90: 	/** Process an incoming question.asked SSE event */
91: 	onQuestion(event: OpenCodeEvent): PendingQuestion | null {
92: 		const props = event.properties as {
93: 			id?: string;
94: 			questions?: Array<{
95: 				question?: string;
96: 				header?: string;
97: 				options?: Array<{ label?: string; description?: string }>;
98: 				multiple?: boolean;
99: 				custom?: boolean;
100: 			}>;
101: 		};
102: 
103: 		if (!props.id || !props.questions) return null;
104: 
105: 		const questions = mapQuestionFields(props.questions);
106: 		const entry: PendingQuestion = {
107: 			toolId: props.id,
108: 			questions,
109: 			timestamp: this.now(),
110: 		};
111: 
112: 		this.pending.set(props.id, entry);
113: 		return entry;
114: 	}
115: 
116: 	/** Process a user's answer from the browser */
117: 	onAnswer(
118: 		toolId: string,
119: 		answers: Record<string, string>,
120: 	): { formatted: string[][] } | null {
121: 		const entry = this.pending.get(toolId);
122: 		if (!entry) return null;
123: 
124: 		const formatted = convertAnswers(answers, entry.questions);
125: 		this.pending.delete(toolId);
126: 		return { formatted };
127: 	}
128: 
129: 	/** Get all pending questions (for replay on reconnect) */
130: 	getPending(): PendingQuestion[] {
131: 		return Array.from(this.pending.values());
132: 	}
133: 
134: 	/** Check for timed-out questions */
135: 	checkTimeouts(): string[] {
136: 		const now = this.now();
137: 		const timedOut: string[] = [];
138: 
139: 		for (const [id, entry] of this.pending) {
140: 			if (now - entry.timestamp >= this.timeoutMs) {
141: 				timedOut.push(id);
142: 				// NOTE: per spec, questions do NOT auto-answer on timeout
143: 				// but we track them for notification purposes
144: 			}
145: 		}
146: 
147: 		return timedOut;
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	/** Remove a question (e.g., after timeout notification) */
151: 	remove(toolId: string): boolean {
152: 		return this.pending.delete(toolId);
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	get size(): number {
156: 		return this.pending.size;
157: 	}
158: }

(End of file - total 158 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: // ─── Relay Stack ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // The complete relay wiring: OpenCode client, SSE consumer, event translator,
3: // WebSocket handler, session manager, permission/question bridges.
4: //
5: // Extracted from skeleton.ts so integration tests exercise the exact same
6: // wiring as production. skeleton.ts is now a thin CLI wrapper around this.
7: 
8: import { stat } from "node:fs/promises";
9: import { createRequire } from "node:module";
10: import { homedir } from "node:os";
11: import { join, resolve } from "node:path";
12: import { type ClientInitDeps, handleClientConnected } from "./client-init.js";
13: import { formatErrorDetail, RelayError } from "./errors.js";
14: import { createTranslator } from "./event-translator.js";
15: import { dispatchMessage, type HandlerDeps } from "./handlers/index.js";
16: import { getClientIp, parseCookies } from "./http-utils.js";
17: import { generateSlug } from "./ipc-protocol.js";
18: import { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
19: import { OpenCodeClient } from "./opencode-client.js";
20: import { PermissionBridge } from "./permission-bridge.js";
21: import { PtyManager, type PtyUpstream } from "./pty-manager.js";
22: import type { PushNotificationManager } from "./push.js";
23: import { QuestionBridge } from "./question-bridge.js";
24: import { RelayServer } from "./server.js";
25: import { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
26: import { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
27: import { SSEConsumer } from "./sse-consumer.js";
28: import { wireSSEConsumer } from "./sse-wiring.js";
29: import type { PartType, ProjectRelayConfig, ToolStatus } from "./types.js";
30: import { WebSocketHandler } from "./ws-handler.js";
31: 
32: // ─── WebSocket library for upstream PTY connections ─────────────────────────
33: const requireWs = createRequire(import.meta.url);
34: const wsLib = requireWs("ws");
35: const WebSocketClass = wsLib.WebSocket as typeof import("ws").WebSocket;
36: type RawData = import("ws").RawData;
37: 
38: /** Per-project relay: all relay components attached to a shared server. */
39: export interface ProjectRelay {
40: 	wsHandler: WebSocketHandler;
41: 	sseConsumer: SSEConsumer;
42: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
43: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
44: 	translator: ReturnType<typeof createTranslator>;
45: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
46: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
47: 	/** Gracefully stop relay components (SSE + WebSocket). Does NOT stop the HTTP server. */
48: 	stop(): Promise<void>;
49: }
50: 
51: // ─── Full Stack Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52: 
53: export interface RelayStackConfig {
54: 	port: number;
55: 	host?: string;
56: 	opencodeUrl: string;
57: 	pin?: string;
58: 	projectDir: string;
59: 	slug: string;
60: 	staticDir?: string;
61: 	/** TLS certificate and key for HTTPS mode */
62: 	tls?: { key: Buffer; cert: Buffer; caRoot?: string };
63: 	/** Session title for the initial session */
64: 	sessionTitle?: string;
65: 	/** Log function — defaults to console.log, pass () => {} to silence */
66: 	log?: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
67: 	/** Optional pre-initialized push notification manager */
68: 	pushManager?: PushNotificationManager;
69: }
70: 
71: // ─── Stack ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72: 
73: export interface RelayStack {
74: 	server: RelayServer;
75: 	wsHandler: WebSocketHandler;
76: 	sseConsumer: SSEConsumer;
77: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
78: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
79: 	translator: ReturnType<typeof createTranslator>;
80: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
81: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
82: 
83: 	/** The port the HTTP server is actually listening on (useful when port=0) */
84: 	getPort(): number;
85: 	/** The base URL of the relay server */
86: 	getBaseUrl(): string;
87: 	/** Stop all components */
88: 	stop(): Promise<void>;
89: }
90: 
91: // ─── Create Per-Project Relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
92: 
93: /**
94:  * Create a per-project relay that attaches to an existing HTTP server.
95:  *
96:  * Sets up all relay components (OpenCode client, SSE consumer, translator,
97:  * session manager, bridges, WebSocket handler) and wires the full event
98:  * pipeline. Does NOT create or manage an HTTP server — the caller owns it.
99:  *
100:  * Used by both `createRelayStack()` (for standalone/skeleton mode) and the
101:  * daemon (which has its own HTTP server).
102:  */
103: export async function createProjectRelay(
104: 	config: ProjectRelayConfig,
105: ): Promise<ProjectRelay> {
106: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
107: 
108: 	// ── Components ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
109: 
110: 	const client = new OpenCodeClient({
111: 		baseUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
112: 		directory: config.noServer ? config.projectDir : undefined,
113: 	});
114: 	const translator = createTranslator();
115: 	const permissionBridge = new PermissionBridge();
116: 	const questionBridge = new QuestionBridge();
117: 	const sessionMgr = new SessionManager({ client });
118: 
119: 	// ── Per-session event cache (identical to claude-relay's doSendAndRecord) ──
120: 	const cacheDir = join(
121: 		config.projectDir ?? process.cwd(),
122: 		".opencode-relay",
123: 		"sessions",
124: 	);
125: 	const messageCache = new MessageCache(cacheDir);
126: 	messageCache.loadFromDisk();
127: 
128: 	// Per-session overrides (agent, model, processing timeout)
129: 	const overrides = new SessionOverrides();
130: 
131: 	// ── PTY sessions with server-side scrollback (claude-relay architecture) ──
132: 	// Each active PTY gets one upstream WebSocket to OpenCode's /pty/:id/connect.
133: 	// Output is buffered server-side (50 KB FIFO per terminal) and broadcast to
134: 	// ALL browser clients. New clients get scrollback replayed on connect.
135: 	// Input from any browser client is forwarded to the shared upstream WS.
136: 	// PTYs persist across browser show/hide toggles — only closed on explicit
137: 	// pty_close (tab X button) or upstream disconnect.
138: 
139: 	const ptyManager = new PtyManager({ log });
140: 
141: 	async function connectPtyUpstream(
142: 		ptyId: string,
143: 		cursor: number = 0,
144: 	): Promise<void> {
145: 		// cursor=0 : replay entire PTY buffer (use for newly-created PTYs so the
146: 		//            initial shell prompt is delivered).
147: 		// cursor=-1: skip buffered data (use when reconnecting to an existing PTY
148: 		//            to avoid replaying stale escape sequences).
149: 		const httpUrl = new URL(
150: 			`/pty/${ptyId}/connect?cursor=${cursor}`,
151: 			config.opencodeUrl,
152: 		);
153: 		const wsUrl = httpUrl.toString().replace(/^http/, "ws");
154: 		const headers = client.getAuthHeaders();
155: 
156: 		return new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
157: 			const upstream = new WebSocketClass(wsUrl, {
158: 				headers,
159: 				// Disable per-message compression for PTY connections.
160: 				// Bun's WS may negotiate permessage-deflate with Node's ws, and
161: 				// decompression bugs or flush mismatches can corrupt binary PTY data.
162: 				perMessageDeflate: false,
163: 				// Skip UTF-8 validation for TEXT frames. Terminal data can contain
164: 				// 8-bit characters (C1 control codes) that are valid in terminal
165: 				// protocols but technically invalid in strict UTF-8. Without this,
166: 				// the ws library would close the connection on such frames.
167: 				skipUTF8Validation: true,
168: 			});
169: 
170: 			// Register session BEFORE any events can fire. This mirrors the
171: 			// original pattern where the PtySession object was created before
172: 			// the WebSocket — prevents the race condition where OpenCode sends
173: 			// replay data in the same TCP segment as the upgrade response
174: 			// (Node.js ws processes both synchronously).
175: 			ptyManager.registerSession(ptyId, upstream as unknown as PtyUpstream);
176: 			let connected = false;
177: 
178: 			const connectTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
179: 				// Remove the session on timeout — it was never successfully opened
180: 				ptyManager.closeSession(ptyId);
181: 				reject(new Error(`Timed out connecting to PTY ${ptyId}`));
182: 			}, 10_000);
183: 
184: 			// Install message handler BEFORE "open" fires.
185: 			upstream.on("message", (data: RawData) => {
186: 				// OpenCode sends cursor metadata as binary: 0x00 + JSON {"cursor":N}.
187: 				// Always convert to Buffer first and check byte 0.
188: 				let buf: Buffer;
189: 				if (Buffer.isBuffer(data)) {
190: 					buf = data;
191: 				} else if (typeof data === "string") {
192: 					buf = Buffer.from(data);
193: 				} else {
194: 					buf = Buffer.from(data as ArrayBuffer);
195: 				}
196: 
197: 				if (buf.length > 0 && buf[0] === 0x00) return;
198: 				const text = buf.toString();
199: 				if (!text) return;
200: 
201: 				// Buffer scrollback via PtyManager (FIFO, 50 KB cap)
202: 				ptyManager.appendScrollback(ptyId, text);
203: 
204: 				// Broadcast to all browser clients
205: 				wsHandler.broadcast({ type: "pty_output", ptyId, data: text });
206: 			});
207: 
208: 			upstream.on("close", () => {
209: 				ptyManager.markExited(ptyId, 0);
210: 				// Only broadcast pty_exited if the session is still tracked.
211: 				// closeSession deletes + broadcasts pty_deleted, so we must
212: 				// not also broadcast pty_exited for the same PTY.  Similarly,
213: 				// if connectPtyUpstream failed the session was never stored.
214: 				if (ptyManager.hasSession(ptyId)) {
215: 					wsHandler.broadcast({
216: 						type: "pty_exited",
217: 						ptyId,
218: 						exitCode: 0,
219: 					});
220: 				}
221: 				log(`   [pty] Upstream closed: ${ptyId}`);
222: 			});
223: 
224: 			upstream.on("error", (err: Error) => {
225: 				if (!connected) {
226: 					clearTimeout(connectTimeout);
227: 					// Remove the failed session
228: 					ptyManager.closeSession(ptyId);
229: 					reject(err);
230: 				}
231: 				log(`   [pty] Upstream error ${ptyId}: ${err.message}`);
232: 			});
233: 
234: 			upstream.on("open", () => {
235: 				connected = true;
236: 				clearTimeout(connectTimeout);
237: 				resolve();
238: 			});
239: 		});
240: 	}
241: 
242: 	// ── Health check ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
243: 
244: 	await client.getHealth();
245: 	log(`   ✓ OpenCode is reachable at ${config.opencodeUrl}`);
246: 
247: 	// ── Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
248: 
249: 	const sessionId = await sessionMgr.initialize(
250: 		config.sessionTitle ?? "Relay Session",
251: 	);
252: 	log(`   ✓ Using session: ${sessionId}`);
253: 
254: 	// ── WebSocket handler ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
255: 
256: 	const wsHandler = new WebSocketHandler(
257: 		config.noServer ? null : config.httpServer,
258: 		{
259: 			// In noServer mode, the daemon's upgrade handler checks auth before
260: 			// calling handleUpgrade(). Skip verifyClient to avoid double auth.
261: 			verifyClient: config.noServer ? undefined : config.verifyClient,
262: 		},
263: 	);
264: 
265: 	const clientInitDeps: ClientInitDeps = {
266: 		wsHandler,
267: 		client,
268: 		sessionMgr,
269: 		messageCache,
270: 		overrides,
271: 		ptyManager,
272: 		permissionBridge,
273: 		questionBridge,
274: 		log,
275: 	};
276: 
277: 	wsHandler.on("client_connected", ({ clientId }) => {
278: 		log(`   [ws] Client connected: ${clientId}`);
279: 		handleClientConnected(clientInitDeps, clientId).catch((err) =>
280: 			log(
281: 				`   [ws] Client init failed for ${clientId}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`,
282: 			),
283: 		);
284: 	});
285: 
286: 	wsHandler.on("client_disconnected", ({ clientId }) => {
287: 		log(`   [ws] Client disconnected: ${clientId}`);
288: 	});
289: 
290: 	// ── SSE consumer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
291: 
292: 	const sseConsumer = new SSEConsumer({
293: 		baseUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
294: 		authHeaders: client.getAuthHeaders(),
295: 	});
296: 
297: 	// ── Wire session manager → WebSocket ────────────────────────────────────
298: 
299: 	sessionMgr.on("broadcast", (msg) => {
300: 		// Augment session_switched with cached events (combined protocol)
301: 		if (msg.type === "session_switched") {
302: 			const switchId = (msg as { id?: string }).id;
303: 			if (switchId) {
304: 				const events = messageCache.getEvents(switchId);
305: 				const hasChatContent =
306: 					events?.some(
307: 						(e) => e.type === "user_message" || e.type === "delta",
308: 					) ?? false;
309: 				if (events && hasChatContent) {
310: 					wsHandler.broadcast({ ...msg, events });
311: 					return;
312: 				}
313: 			}
314: 		}
315: 		wsHandler.broadcast(msg);
316: 	});
317: 	sessionMgr.on("session_changed", async ({ sessionId: sid }) => {
318: 		translator.reset();
319: 		// Rebuild translator state from REST API so it knows which parts already
320: 		// exist in the session we're switching to (prevents duplicate tool_start).
321: 		try {
322: 			const messages = await client.getMessages(sid);
323: 			const parts: Array<{
324: 				parts?: Array<{
325: 					id: string;
326: 					type: PartType;
327: 					state?: { status?: ToolStatus };
328: 				}>;
329: 			}> = messages.map((m) => ({
330: 				parts: (m as { parts?: unknown[] }).parts as
331: 					| Array<{
332: 							id: string;
333: 							type: PartType;
334: 							state?: { status?: ToolStatus };
335: 					  }>
336: 					| undefined,
337: 			}));
338: 			translator.rebuildStateFromHistory(parts);
339: 		} catch {
340: 			// Non-fatal — translator will treat all parts as new
341: 		}
342: 	});
343: 
344: 	// ── Handle incoming messages from browser ───────────────────────────────
345: 	// Messages are serialized: each handler completes before the next starts.
346: 	// Without this, concurrent async handlers (e.g. new_session + switch_session)
347: 	// race — createSession's broadcast can arrive on the client AFTER
348: 	// a switch_session reply, clearing messages the user just switched to.
349: 
350: 	const handlerDeps: HandlerDeps = {
351: 		wsHandler,
352: 		client,
353: 		sessionMgr,
354: 		messageCache,
355: 		permissionBridge,
356: 		questionBridge,
357: 		overrides,
358: 		ptyManager,
359: 		config,
360: 		log,
361: 		connectPtyUpstream,
362: 	};
363: 
364: 	let _messageQueue = Promise.resolve();
365: 
366: 	wsHandler.on("message", ({ clientId, handler, payload }) => {
367: 		_messageQueue = _messageQueue.then(async () => {
368: 			try {
369: 				await dispatchMessage(handlerDeps, clientId, handler, payload);
370: 			} catch (err) {
371: 				log(`   [ws] Error handling ${handler}:`, formatErrorDetail(err));
372: 				wsHandler.sendTo(
373: 					clientId,
374: 					RelayError.fromCaught(err, "HANDLER_ERROR").toMessage(),
375: 				);
376: 			}
377: 		});
378: 	});
379: 
380: 	// ── SSE event wiring (translate → filter → cache → broadcast) ──────────
381: 
382: 	wireSSEConsumer(
383: 		{
384: 			translator,
385: 			sessionMgr,
386: 			messageCache,
387: 			permissionBridge,
388: 			questionBridge,
389: 			overrides,
390: 			wsHandler,
391: 			pushManager: config.pushManager,
392: 			log,
393: 		},
394: 		sseConsumer,
395: 	);
396: 
397: 	await sseConsumer.connect();
398: 
399: 	// ── Permission/question timeout checks ──────────────────────────────────
400: 
401: 	const timeoutTimer = setInterval(() => {
402: 		const timedOutPerms = permissionBridge.checkTimeouts();
403: 		for (const id of timedOutPerms) {
404: 			wsHandler.broadcast({
405: 				type: "permission_resolved",
406: 				requestId: id,
407: 				decision: "timeout",
408: 			});
409: 		}
410: 		const timedOutQuestions = questionBridge.checkTimeouts();
411: 		for (const id of timedOutQuestions) {
412: 			questionBridge.remove(id);
413: 			wsHandler.broadcast({ type: "ask_user_resolved", toolId: id });
414: 		}
415: 	}, 30_000);
416: 
417: 	// Don't let the timer keep the process alive
418: 	if (
419: 		timeoutTimer &&
420: 		typeof timeoutTimer === "object" &&
421: 		"unref" in timeoutTimer
422: 	) {
423: 		timeoutTimer.unref();
424: 	}
425: 
426: 	// ── Return project relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
427: 
428: 	return {
429: 		wsHandler,
430: 		sseConsumer,
431: 		client,
432: 		sessionMgr,
433: 		translator,
434: 		permissionBridge,
435: 		questionBridge,
436: 
437: 		async stop() {
438: 			clearInterval(timeoutTimer);
439: 			overrides.dispose();
440: 			ptyManager.closeAll();
441: 			await sseConsumer.disconnect();
442: 			wsHandler.close();
443: 		},
444: 	};
445: }
446: 
447: // ─── Create Full Stack (Server + Relay) ─────────────────────────────────────
448: 
449: /**
450:  * Create a full relay stack with its own HTTP server.
451:  *
452:  * Creates a RelayServer, registers the project, starts the server, then
453:  * delegates to `createProjectRelay()` for all relay wiring. Used by
454:  * skeleton.ts for standalone operation.
455:  */
456: export async function createRelayStack(
457: 	config: RelayStackConfig,
458: ): Promise<RelayStack> {
459: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
460: 
461: 	// ── Push notification manager ────────────────────────────────────────────
462: 
463: 	let pushMgr: PushNotificationManager | undefined = config.pushManager;
464: 	if (!pushMgr) {
465: 		try {
466: 			const { PushNotificationManager } = await import("./push.js");
467: 			pushMgr = new PushNotificationManager();
468: 			await pushMgr.init();
469: 		} catch {
470: 			pushMgr = undefined;
471: 		}
472: 	}
473: 
474: 	// ── HTTP server ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
475: 
476: 	const server = new RelayServer({
477: 		port: config.port,
478: 		host: config.host,
479: 		pin: config.pin || undefined,
480: 		staticDir: config.staticDir,
481: 		tls: config.tls,
482: 		pushManager: pushMgr,
483: 	});
484: 
485: 	server.addProject({
486: 		slug: config.slug,
487: 		directory: config.projectDir,
488: 		title: config.slug,
489: 	});
490: 
491: 	await server.start();
492: 
493: 	const maybeServer = server.getHttpServer();
494: 	if (!maybeServer) {
495: 		throw new Error("HTTP server not available after start()");
496: 	}
497: 	// Assign to a fresh const so TypeScript narrows to non-null in closures.
498: 	const httpServer = maybeServer;
499: 
500: 	// ── Multi-project relay management ──────────────────────────────────────
501: 	// All relays use noServer mode. A single upgrade handler routes WebSocket
502: 	// connections to the correct relay by URL path (/ws → initial, /p/{slug}/ws → project).
503: 	// This matches the daemon pattern and allows dynamic project addition.
504: 
505: 	const relays = new Map<string, ProjectRelay>();
506: 	const pendingSlugs = new Set<string>();
507: 
508: 	const getProjectList = () =>
509: 		server.getProjects().map((p) => ({
510: 			slug: p.slug,
511: 			title: p.title,
512: 			directory: p.directory,
513: 		}));
514: 
515: 	/** Create a new project relay and register it. */
516: 	async function addProjectRelay(
517: 		directory: string,
518: 	): Promise<{ slug: string; title: string; directory: string }> {
519: 		// Expand ~ and resolve to absolute path
520: 		if (directory.startsWith("~/") || directory === "~") {
521: 			directory = directory.replace("~", homedir());
522: 		}
523: 		directory = resolve(directory);
524: 
525: 		// Check if directory is already registered
526: 		for (const p of server.getProjects()) {
527: 			if (p.directory === directory) {
528: 				return { slug: p.slug, title: p.title, directory: p.directory };
529: 			}
530: 		}
531: 
532: 		// Validate directory exists on disk
533: 		const dirStat = await stat(directory).catch(() => null);
534: 		if (!dirStat?.isDirectory()) {
535: 			throw new Error(
536: 				dirStat
537: 					? `Not a directory: ${directory}`
538: 					: `Directory does not exist: ${directory}`,
539: 			);
540: 		}
541: 
542: 		const existingSlugs = new Set(relays.keys());
543: 		const slug = generateSlug(directory, existingSlugs);
544: 		const parts = directory.replace(/\\/g, "/").split("/").filter(Boolean);
545: 		const title = parts[parts.length - 1] ?? "project";
546: 
547: 		// Guard against concurrent creation for the same slug
548: 		if (relays.has(slug) || pendingSlugs.has(slug)) {
549: 			const existing = relays.get(slug);
550: 			if (existing) return { slug, title, directory };
551: 			throw new Error(`Relay for ${directory} is still being created`);
552: 		}
553: 
554: 		pendingSlugs.add(slug);
555: 		try {
556: 			// Create relay FIRST — if this throws, nothing is registered
557: 			const newRelay = await createProjectRelay({
558: 				httpServer,
559: 				opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
560: 				projectDir: directory,
561: 				slug,
562: 				noServer: true,
563: 				sessionTitle: config.sessionTitle,
564: 				log,
565: 				getProjects: getProjectList,
566: 				addProject: addProjectRelay,
567: 				pushManager: pushMgr,
568: 			});
569: 
570: 			// Only register AFTER relay is successfully created
571: 			relays.set(slug, newRelay);
572: 			server.addProject({ slug, directory, title });
573: 
574: 			log(`   [project] Added: ${title} (${slug}) → ${directory}`);
575: 		} catch (err) {
576: 			// Clean up on failure — no zombie entries
577: 			relays.delete(slug);
578: 			log(`   [project] Failed to add ${directory}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`);
579: 			throw err;
580: 		} finally {
581: 			pendingSlugs.delete(slug);
582: 		}
583: 
584: 		return { slug, title, directory };
585: 	}
586: 
587: 	// ── Initial project relay ───────────────────────────────────────────────
588: 
589: 	const relay = await createProjectRelay({
590: 		httpServer,
591: 		opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
592: 		projectDir: config.projectDir,
593: 		slug: config.slug,
594: 		sessionTitle: config.sessionTitle,
595: 		log,
596: 		noServer: true,
597: 		getProjects: getProjectList,
598: 		addProject: addProjectRelay,
599: 		pushManager: pushMgr,
600: 	});
601: 	relays.set(config.slug, relay);
602: 
603: 	// ── WebSocket upgrade handler ───────────────────────────────────────────
604: 	// Routes connections by URL: /p/{slug}/ws → project relay, /ws → initial relay.
605: 	// Also checks auth when a PIN is configured (fixes pre-existing gap where
606: 	// standalone WS connections bypassed PIN auth).
607: 
608: 	httpServer.on("upgrade", (req, socket, head) => {
609: 		// Auth check (mirrors server.ts private checkAuth)
610: 		const auth = server.getAuth();
611: 		if (auth.hasPin()) {
612: 			const cookies = parseCookies(req.headers.cookie ?? "");
613: 			const sessionCookie = cookies.relay_session;
614: 			const cookieOk = sessionCookie
615: 				? auth.validateCookie(sessionCookie)
616: 				: false;
617: 			if (!cookieOk) {
618: 				const pinHeader = req.headers["x-relay-pin"];
619: 				const pinOk =
620: 					typeof pinHeader === "string" &&
621: 					auth.authenticate(pinHeader, getClientIp(req)).ok;
622: 				if (!pinOk) {
623: 					socket.destroy();
624: 					return;
625: 				}
626: 			}
627: 		}
628: 
629: 		// Route /p/{slug}/ws → project relay
630: 		const projectMatch = req.url?.match(/^\/p\/([^/]+)\/ws(?:\?|$)/);
631: 		if (projectMatch) {
632: 			const target = relays.get(projectMatch[1]!);
633: 			if (target) {
634: 				target.wsHandler.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head);
635: 			} else {
636: 				socket.destroy();
637: 			}
638: 			return;
639: 		}
640: 
641: 		// Route /ws → initial relay
642: 		if (req.url === "/ws" || req.url?.startsWith("/ws?")) {
643: 			relay.wsHandler.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head);
644: 			return;
645: 		}
646:
